# Thread Insights 2026 (Per Project)

The project-specific record of which 2026 trends, threads, and emerging patterns this project deliberately engaged with — and which it deliberately rejected. Reference: `.claude/skills/award-website-os/references/54-thread-insights-2026.md` (if present), `60-deep-awwwards-investigation-2026.md`.

The point of this doc is to keep the project from looking dated in 12 months while also keeping it from looking like every other 2026 site.

| <FILL IN — e.g. OKLCH color tokens> | <FILL IN> | <FILL IN> |
| <FILL IN — e.g. R3F + KTX2 hero> | <FILL IN> | <FILL IN> |

## "Will this look dated in 18 months?" check

For each major design decision, mark:

| Decision | Dating risk (L/M/H) | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Hero composition | <FILL IN> | <FILL IN> |
| Type system | <FILL IN> | <FILL IN> |
| Color treatment | <FILL IN> | <FILL IN> |
| Motion language | <FILL IN> | <FILL IN> |
| 3D usage (if any) | <FILL IN> | <FILL IN> |

Any "H" must be either justified or replaced.

## What this project does that the category isn't doing yet

<FILL IN — the move ahead of the curve. If there isn't one, the project is following, not leading.>

## What this project does NOT do that the category is doing

<FILL IN — the deliberate omission. If there isn't one, the project lacks restraint.>

## Things we'd revisit in 6 months

<FILL IN — patterns that are too new to commit to now, but worth a follow-up>
